get.description:
Returns the Supabase Google OAuth URL.
Frontend should redirect the user's browser to the returned URL.
`redirect_to` is where Supabase sends the user after Google consent (your frontend callback page).

post.description:
Exchange the authorization code from Supabase Google OAuth callback for a session.
The frontend calls this after being redirected back from Google with the `code` param.
Returns access_token + refresh_token + user.
Get a new access_token using a refresh_token.

get.description: Get current user profile with branch_id. Requires access_token.

Frontend uses this to adapt UI (hide buttons, cap discount sliders, etc.).

get.description:

Close an accounting period. No new journal entries can be created
for dates within this period after closing.

get.description: List all accounting periods (open, closed, locked).
List all Chart of Accounts for the restaurant.
Return the Chart of Accounts as a hierarchical tree.

T-account ledger for a single CoA account.
Returns all movements with running balance.

Profit & Loss (Income Statement) — derived ONLY from journal_lines.

Revenue  = net credit on revenue accounts
Expenses = net debit on expense accounts
Net Income = Revenue − Expenses
Cash inflow vs outflow summary (merges legacy + double-entry rows).
Trial Balance — debit/credit totals per account. Balanced books = totals match.
Balance Sheet — Assets = Liabilities + Equity.

Record a payment gateway settlement.

Creates journal entries:

get.description:

Positive = money captured by gateways but not yet settled to bank.
Find online payments not yet included in any settlement batch.

post.description:
Create a custom accounting rule.

Rules override the default hardcoded journal entry patterns.
When an event fires, the engine checks custom rules first (by priority),
and falls back to defaults if no rule matches.

get.description: Income Statement (P&L) — Revenue, Expenses, Net Income for a period.
Customer Aging Report — 30/60/90+ day AR buckets.

Customer Statement — full ledger of a customer's transactions + balance.
Supplier Statement — full ledger of a supplier's transactions + balance.

Derived from journal entries touching cash, bank, and card accounts.

Audit Drilldown — trace any number back to its source journal entries.

Usage:
  - From trial balance → pass account_id to see all entries for that account
  - From P&L line → pass reference_type to see all entries of that type
  - From specific transaction → pass reference_id to see the exact entry
  - CA asks "show me origin of this number" → this endpoint answers it

get.description:
Accounting Integrity Validator — run full consistency check.

Checks:
  1. Trial balance: sum(debit) == sum(credit) globally
  2. Entry balance: every individual journal entry balances
  3. Orphan lines: journal_lines without parent entry
  4. Broken account refs: lines referencing deleted accounts
  5. Reversal integrity: reversed entries properly linked

Returns all_passed=true if system is fully consistent.

Drill into any number back to its source journal entries.

Step 1: Initialize daily closing — system computes expected cash/card/UPI
from the ledger for the given date.

Step 2: Cashier enters actual counted amounts.
Returns difference and mismatch flag.
Step 3: Manager closes the day — locks it, produces summary.
